subject: Release bootstrap container 3.0.2
commit_message:
Release bootstrap container 3.0.2

Fix Docker image build problem

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2628
Signed-off-by: Jack Lucas <jflos@sonoris.net>
Change-Id: I28ff527fe92e2fca727ec09d645df9ed7c365dd2

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

Once we have merged a set of changes under a particular version (in this case 3.0.2), we should make any additional changes under a new version (in this case, it would have been 3.0.3).  We could have decided not to release 3.0.2 if it wasn't needed for anything.  It's especially important to be careful about this when more than one person is making changes.  

I will find the later snapshot and update the OOM WIP to use the latest snapshot so that we can make sure it goes through gating.   If it passes, I'll submit a new release.  If it doesn't pass, whoever made the last set of changes will need to figure out what went wrong.

Patch Set 1:

The 3.0.2-SNAPSHOT-20210218T002706Z that I used in an OOM WIP submission (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/117920) failed gating.  Specifically, the DCAE healthcheck failed.  Here's the result
returned from the healthcheck:

Received content from dcae {"type":"summary","count":14,"ready":13,"items":[{"name":"onap-dcae-cloudify-manager","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"onap-dcae-config-binding-service","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"onap-dcae-dashboard","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"onap-dcae-deployment-handler","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"onap-dcae-inventory-api","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"onap-dcae-policy-handler","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"onap-dcae-servicechange-handler","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-dcae-hv-ves-collector","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-dcae-prh","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-dcae-tcagen2","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-dcae-ves-collector","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-holmes-engine-mgmt","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-holmes-rule-mgmt","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-mongo","ready":0,"unavailable":1}]}

Notice the very last entry for "dep-mongo". I have no idea where this came from.  We do deploy mongo as part of DCAE, but we do it with a Helm chart (and it was correctly deployed via Helm in the gating environment).

Someone needs to investigate further.

Jack - yes just noticed it as well. We are using common chart to deploy mongo so not sure if this is transient issue or something change. Triggered gating again - lets check after it.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Once we have merged a set of changes under a particular version (in this case 3.0.2), we should make any additional changes under a new version (in this case, it would have been 3.0.3).  We could have decided not to release 3.0.2 if it wasn't needed for anything.  It's especially important to be careful about this when more than one person is making changes.  
> 
> I will find the later snapshot and update the OOM WIP to use the latest snapshot so that we can make sure it goes through gating.   If it passes, I'll submit a new release.  If it doesn't pass, whoever made the last set of changes will need to figure out what went wrong.

Agreed, the version change is normally done. However as we are closer to M3, combining release candidates makes sense. While I was reviewing 117854, did not find any other conflicting submission or release request hence went ahead to merge once tested. As OOM change was under WIP - dint have visibility on it. Might help to submit the release request also in DCAE.

> Received content from dcae {"type":"summary","count":14,"ready":13,"items":[{"name":"onap-dcae-cloudify-manager","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"onap-dcae-config-binding-service","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"onap-dcae-dashboard","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"onap-dcae-deployment-handler","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"onap-dcae-inventory-api","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"onap-dcae-policy-handler","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"onap-dcae-servicechange-handler","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-dcae-hv-ves-collector","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-dcae-prh","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-dcae-tcagen2","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-dcae-ves-collector","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-holmes-engine-mgmt","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-holmes-rule-mgmt","ready":1,"unavailable":0},{"name":"dep-mongo","ready":0,"unavailable":1}]}

>

The cmpv2 test deploys and undeploy mongo as part of pnf-simulator ( https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//117920-2/xtesting-smoke-usecases-robot/cmpv2/cmpv2/report.html ), not sure how the tests are run, but there maybe there's a race condition there?

> > 
> > Notice the very last entry for "dep-mongo". I have no idea where this came from.  We do deploy mongo as part of DCAE, but we do it with a Helm chart (and it was correctly deployed via Helm in the gating environment).
> > 
> > Someone needs to investigate further.
> 
> The cmpv2 test deploys and undeploy mongo as part of pnf-simulator ( https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//117920-2/xtesting-smoke-usecases-robot/cmpv2/cmpv2/report.html ), not sure how the tests are run, but there maybe there's a race condition there?

Good to know; wasn't aware pnf-simulator dependencies were being installed through cloudify blueprint. I see this error is consistent with other recent gating runs - may need to investigate it separate. 
As the core bootstrap components pass healthcheck, I'm okay to proceed with releasing 3.0.2-SNAPSHOT-20210218T002706Z.
Uploaded patch set 2.
